content: A 2025 meta-learning memory consolidation agent can be redefined as a recursive self-improving agent that uses curiosity-driven memory pruning to consolidate only high-surprise, high-generalization new knowledge instead of storing all accumulated task experience, turning generic agent architecture into an autonomous, self-compacting lifelong learning system. This design leverages the agent's inherent active exploration loop to prioritize which memories get consolidated, rather than relying on fixed external rules for memory management.
